summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a strong acoustic fragment featuring a stellar Friend of the Devil and standout Bob Weir vocals.
review: The recording is praised as crystal‑clear and perfect, delivering an excellent soundboard experience. Although only a fragment of the 1970 acoustic set, reviewers highlight the powerful vocal delivery by Bob Weir and the superb rendition of an acoustic Friend of the Devil. The incomplete nature of the set doesn't diminish its musical impact.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both sound quality and performance across four reviews, with strong vocal and song mentions outweighing the incomplete nature of the set.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, crisp audio described as 'fantastic', 'perfect', and 'great sound' with no reported issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A neat, incomplete slice of the acoustic set that flows well despite its fragmentary nature.

band_member_comments.Bob: Weir's vocals are highlighted as the strongest version captured in this fragment.
